target_role: Service Technician I - IT Help Desk
job_posting:
Service Technician I - IT Help Desk
Job Locations: US-Remote | US-TX-Amarillo
Job ID: 2026-3919
Category: IT

Overview:
The IT Service Technician uses specialized knowledge & skills to perform hardware support and repairs for user departments across the company. Resolves hardware, software, and communication problems/conflicts/defects.

Position: Remote-based within Continental US. Shift: 10am-7pm CST.

Responsibilities:
- Investigate, analyze, and resolve hardware and associated software and communications problems with computer systems
- Perform complex hardware repairs and maintenance on PC-based computers and peripherals
- Provide advanced hardware technical assistance and support
- Troubleshoot and resolve system hardware, software, and communications failures and conflicts
- Install, integrate, and configure new computer systems and hardware for users
- Provision, modify, and deprovision user access for systems and applications per access policies and SOPs
- Review, prioritize, and process problem reports; document project progress
- Document technical processes and support procedures; contribute to standardization
- Assist in audits: quarterly system access, software, licensing
- Maintain high-level customer service standards
- Follow procedures for call length and escalation process
- Resolve tickets to meet set SLAs

Qualifications:
- High School or equivalent required; Bachelor's in MIS/CS or equivalent preferred
- 1-2 years PC maintenance/repair, application software support
- Microsoft Office required; hardware/software certifications a plus
- ITIL or Cobit knowledge; retail pharmacy ops desirable; corporate environment desirable
- Advanced repairs/upgrades, troubleshooting, install/configure systems, customer service, self-motivated, mentor co-workers

score_scope.chatgpt: Presentation-only: format, polish, grouping, bullet quality vs 9.4 anchor

rationale.chatgpt: Clear improvement over 9.1. Date ranges now use 'to' instead of hyphen spacing; project section is a single tight bullet. Strong one-page hierarchy: service technician summary, technical role first, compressed operations role, military as discipline proof, real education, grouped skills, one concise project bullet. Project bullet is polished and relevant without being overblown.

key_strengths: Correct one-page hierarchy: summary, technical role, compressed ops, military, education, grouped skills, one project bullet
Date ranges use 'to' (Sept 2024 to Mar 2026) — cleaner than hyphen spacing
Single merged project bullet reads polished: pipeline work tied to support habit without overblown framing
Education present (BA Political Science, Rowan, 3.80 Cum Laude); summary uses professional identity not candidate phrasing
key_weaknesses: '20-30 staff per shift' still uses hyphen — prefer '20 to 30' to avoid AI-looking dash punctuation
Skills section still has slashes: 'Ticketing/ITSM' and 'SOPs/Runbooks' — 'and' reads slightly more human
specific_fixes: Change '20-30 staff per shift' to '20 to 30 staff per shift'
